findings.Problem: In-repo quickstart docs will reference personas authored in a separate repository without a stated coordination gate
findings.Fix: Add an acceptance criterion that docs changes merge only after the `atcr/personas` index.json is live
findings.Evidence: "Cross-repo note: ... Tasks 1-2 ... in the separate `atcr/personas` community repo ... Only Task 3 (documentation) is an in-repo change"

findings.Problem: Cross-repo content tasks (1-2) have no verifiable acceptance path within this repo
findings.Fix: Add a CI check or smoke test that validates the external personas repo index.json is fetchable and parsable
findings.Evidence: "Tasks 1–2 below) is authored and published in the separate atcr/personas community repo, not in this codebase"

findings.Problem: No error-path coverage for persona install failure when external repo is unreachable
findings.Fix: Document expected failure modes or add a negative test case for network-unreachable install
findings.Evidence: "fetching persona YAML from a configurable URL"

findings.Problem: Section header "Items needing user confirmation (6)" is a contract violation — all 6 listed items are prefixed with "✅" and conclude "Resolved", so none actually need user confirmation
findings.Fix: Rename the section to "Resolved items" or "Items previously needing confirmation (6, all resolved)" so the header matches the content
findings.Evidence: ### Items needing user confirmation (6) ... - ✅ **Missed component(s) ...:** Resolved. ... - ✅ **Cross-system hosting gap ...:** Resolved. ...

findings.Problem: COMPONENTS_TOUCHED includes `internal/registry` as an in-repo component but the plan's refinements explicitly dropped all registry work — line 44 states "no longer touches `ATCR_REGISTRY_URL` or `registry.yaml` hosting" and line 15 says "Only Task 3 (documentation) is an in-repo change"
findings.Fix: Remove `internal/registry` from COMPONENTS_TOUCHED and correct COMPONENT_COUNT from 2 to 1
findings.Evidence: COMPONENTS_TOUCHED: [internal/registry, docs] — contradicted by line 44: "this epic no longer touches `ATCR_REGISTRY_URL` or `registry.yaml` hosting"

findings.Problem: Refinement metadata lists `internal/registry` as a touched component despite plan limiting in-repo work to docs
findings.Fix: Reconcile `COMPONENTS_TOUCHED` with the actual scope or document why registry code must change

findings.Problem: Epic title "Default Model-Tuned Community Personas" contradicts the filename "19.6_community_registry_hub.md"
findings.Fix: Rename file to match the new focus on personas rather than a registry hub
findings.Evidence: filename is registry_hub but title is Model-Tuned Community Personas

findings.Problem: File path retains "registry_hub" naming after epic was rescoped to personas
findings.Fix: Rename file to `19.6_community_personas.md` to match the new content
findings.Evidence: path `.planning/epics/active/19.6_community_registry_hub.md` vs title "Default Model-Tuned Community Personas"

findings.Problem: Acceptance criteria reference fixture convention but no test for fixture validity against schema
findings.Fix: Add a schema-validation step in the persona-authoring checklist or CI
findings.Evidence: "Each new persona has a passing fixture per the existing contribution checklist"

findings.Problem: Refinements timestamp reads "2026-07-05 04:14:59PM" — date is in the future and the 12-hour suffix on a precise timestamp is non-standard
findings.Fix: Verify intended date (likely 2025-07-05) and normalize to ISO 8601 (`2025-07-05T16:14:59`) so the refinement record is grep-friendly and not future-dated
findings.Evidence: ### Refinements (2026-07-05)\n\nThis section records findings from `/refine-epic` run on July 05, 2026 04:14:59PM.

findings.Problem: "Auto-applied corrections (0) ... No automatic corrections were applied." section is empty boilerplate that adds no information
findings.Fix: Delete the subsection, or merge into the "Verification context" block if a count field is needed
findings.Evidence: ### Auto-applied corrections (0)\n\nNo automatic corrections were applied.
